Fun little AR pet, like a VR tamagotchi. Watching it jump up on my furniture was fun, as was the food eating competition. I enjoyed the "play dates" with other pets. The door was a bit of a surprise and took some figuring out without instructions. It could have more audio feedback coming from the pet. Dogs bark, cats meow. This guy was pretty quiet outside of it's speech bubbles. Which was nice, just almost too quiet :D. Perhaps it could be an option/choice within the game where you can choose which you prefer, or how much you want it to do that.

reviews.review_title: How can I reset my installation?
reviews.review_description: I mapped my environment the first time, but after that even by reinstall the app, that map still there. The problem is that I moved my table location, I need to reset so it will map my new env(room) layout again. I need that, due I am enjoying working using Remote Desktop and having dot running in parallel.

So the tracking is mid (meta problem not a game problem) fairly glitchy but not terrible I can't figure out the whole "door" thing you should probably also be subscribed to me (˵ ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°˵) but yeah I love the cosmetics!

I like this game and my daughter loves Dot too. It is very relaxing.

My suggestion would be having Dot smaller and it should grow as time goes by then make it fat when we overfeed it and it should make  some sort of sound when it's hungry. It would be great also if we can teach Dot some tricks.

Saw there was an update, gave niantic another chance against my better judgement.
I can now happily say that this isn't just a glorified tech demo anymore: it's a glitchy, broken and unplayable mess.
Constant visual stuttering, no respect to boundary so assets are entirely outside of my house on a floor that does not exist, hand tracking is less functional now than ever, there's no way to recenter yourself or the customization object so if you cant reach it you're just screwed.
It's pretty wild that a multi billion dollar corporation like Niantic with direct backing from an even bigger corp like google can't make even a half respectable AR game. I've seen college students do more on this platform with less.
This is genuinely unacceptable. If you were an indie, this would be an entirely different story.
Did you learn anything from the Shapers, or nah?

developer_response.body: Sad that you are not enjoying the free experience. It sounds like you may need to rescan your room and make sure boundary is off, as this uses the passthrough camera only. There is no need to recenter. If the Arcade is spawning outside of reach, look at your hands and clap 3 times. It will bring the machine to you.

reviews.review_title: It worked but also did not
reviews.review_description: When my brother got in, he said it was working for him and he was playing with the little dots but when I got in, I restarted my headset and put it out of the game a couple times and it still didn’t work. My daughter was no where to be seen I didn’t know where it was. I looked around my living room, but it still wasn’t there. I was wondering if my daughter was going to be hiding in a little place but my dot wasn’t ￼.

The Quest 3 has been out for over a year and a half and this is only the second app I’ve seen that uses its enhanced MR capabilities, (the laser scanner), so I have to give it points for that. Until Dot came along I feared that First Encounters was the only Q3 MR app I’d ever see.

Without the scanner MR just feels like a 3D overlay.

Dot shows great promise but it’s still a bit rough.

The worst aspect is there are no instructions at all.

I’m not sure how Dot Ball or the eating contest works.  For example I can eat the fruit but my score doesn’t change. I’m not sure what the ball game is about, I managed to load and fire the ball gun and Dot interacts with it, but I have no idea what the rules of the game are.

The interactions are a little glitchy. Sometimes bringing food to your mouth triggers eating, sometimes it doesn’t. Loading and firing the ball gun is very hit or miss, (pun intended). Often the gun doesn’t respond.

A basic tutorial would make the experience a lot better.

Handling Dot and using the laser pointer works very well though. I get a bit of “phantom  haptics” in my fingertips sometimes while holding Dot which is very surreal.  It’s an amazing illusion.

Wishlist Items:

It would be great if you could disable the blue outlines around your hands.

The machine is nicely designed but I’d prefer a thin door-like portal over the refrigerator sized unit. It takes up most of my play space.  I’m just going to shove it through a door or wall the next time I play.

I’m looking forward to seeing how this progresses. I know Niantic is already working with Meta’s new Passthrough Camera API. I hope they’re planning to use this in Dot.  It would be fantastic to see Dot interacting with moving objects in the environment rather than just my hands and the static scanner mesh.

Very cute virtual pet. Love dipping him in the paint bucket to change his colour! It’s very simple. Love how he runs around the floor. Wish I could make the boundary bigger so he could follow me around the place. It works great for me. Anyone who wants a cute pet should get this ❤️

Experiences like this are what we've been promised since the advent of mixed reality / augmented reality, and while Dot doesn't really have a whole lot of depth to it, it is a good look at what a dev might bring with a properly funded feature-complete app. Anyone who remembers "EyePet" for the PlayStation 3 can see the possibilities.
